notes: Re-audit of 17BD invalidates the prior C4:CE36 anchor claim: that byte pattern is a same-bank JSR inside bank C4 and therefore targets C4:17BD, not C3:17BD.
Direct pattern search finds no JSL/JML hits to C3:17BD and no same-bank C3 JSR/JMP hits either, so 17BD currently lacks a valid caller anchor.

Linear inspection across 17BD..1816 remains opcode-looking but does not stabilize into one trustworthy executable owner under cautious analysis, so the span is better frozen as mixed inline code-looking data than forced into fake code.
The next live seam is the already-isolated 1817 lane, but its external-entry story should be re-validated from real caller context rather than trusting raw byte-pattern matches alone.
